mnemonics: Noise Filtering Is Simply Cleaning Visual Information
keyPoints: Image restoration aims to recover an original image from a degraded version.
Common degradation models include noise (e.g., Gaussian, salt-and-pepper) and blur.
Reconstruction techniques use algorithms like inverse filtering or Wiener filtering to estimate the original image.
commonMistakes: Assuming restoration can perfectly recover an image lost to extreme noise or blur.
Confusing image restoration (repairing an image) with image enhancement (improving visual appearance).
Applying the wrong filter for a specific type of noise (e.g., using a median filter for Gaussian noise).
